description: Edward William Godwin was an innovative interior decorator and designer of furniture, textiles, and theater sets. He designed his first ebonized sideboard—this is a variation—for his dining room in 1867. It represented a shift away from the weight of contemporary Gothic Revival aesthetics and toward a spare style that gained Godwin some notable contemporary clients, such as James McNeill Whistler and Oscar Wilde.
artist_info:
Designed by Edward William Godwin (English
1833-1886)
Made by William Watt (English, 1834-1885)
London
title: Sideboard
